---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size60[.center[
Case Studies:

Can terrorism succeed?
]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. ...

<br>



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-swirls.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle, center

.size60[
Bring to class a piece of evidence showing terrorism (e.g. a terrorist group, a terrorist threat or a specific terror attack) changing the behavior of a target audience (e.g. society, community, government) in some specific way.
]

???

### Everybody ready to go?

<br>

In a moment I'll have everybody introduce their cases.

Take notes as you listen because we have a goal for today.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-swirls.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size55[
Under what conditions are terrorists more or less likely to impact the target audience?

- e.g. what criteria predict terrorist success?
]

???

As you listen make sure to keep this goal in mind!

### Questions?

Ok, let's introduce all the cases.

Try to be specific:

+ What case did you bring?

+ Why was it a "success"?

<br>

*Break into small groups*

Discuss the cases and build your list of criteria

<br>

*Report Back and ON BOARD*

### Bottom line, how and when does terrorism matter?

### Under what conditions are terrorists more or less likely to impact the target audience?

<br>

### Are we comfortable saying that impact equals effectiveness? 

#### - Why or why not?
